title: TSS transformation of non-competent E. coli cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5ad2r","text":"Transformation \u0026 Storage Solution (2X TSS) enables researchers to take advantage of the simple system described by Chung et al., 1989 (DOI:10.1073/pnas.86.7.2172)  for the transformation of  E. coli cells. Early log-phase cells are suspended in 1X TSS, a solution containing polyethylene glycol, dimethyl sulfoxide, and divalent cations in a bacterial growth medium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":139,"length":23},{"style":"italic","offset":191,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":204,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":139,"length":22}],"data":[]},{"key":"6mecp","text":"This protocol is an alternative to the transformation method with chemically CaCl2-competent cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":81,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f8tlv","text":" Advantage: You can use any fresh E. coli cells","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":34,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"94ufv","text":" Disadvantage: Low efficiency. Use only for transformation of plasmids, not for ligation mixtures in molecular cloning. It is possible to transform at least two plasmids at the same time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"15qfm","text":"CaCl2-Transformation does not work for E. coli strains W3110 and W3110 Z1, thus TSS transformation is a method of choice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":4,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":39,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"FDE5CEA86D1511EAB79C0242AC110003","url":"https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.86.7.2172"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"a4m68","text":"2 x TSS (Transformation and Storage Solution):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d1tb9","text":"20 g PEG (3350 or 8000) 20% (w/v)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e8hbj","text":"10 mL DMSO, 10% (v/v)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ti3f","text":"2,03 g MgCl2 * 6H2O 100 mM","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":11,"length":2},{"style":"sub","offset":17,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9gb0j","text":"ad 100mL LB medium (→ pH 6.5)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bj49r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9h351","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqet6","text":"prepare solution without DMSO and autoclave","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"an40s","text":"add DMSO ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cv8n1","text":"make 10 ml aliquots and store at 4 °C","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inoculate 3 ml LB-medium with colonies from a fresh agar plate.
Incubate at 37 °C and 230 rpm for 1.5 to 2.0 h (exponential growth). Culture should become turbid. 02:00:00

Meanwhile prepare 1.5 ml tubes with 200 µl of 2x TSS-buffer and keep on ice. Add 200 µl cells and 0.5 - 1.0 µl Plasmid. Vortex and incubate on ice for 20 - 30 min. Longer incubation time will reduce transformation efficiency! 00:20:00
Incubate 45-60 min at 37 °C on the Thermomixer with shaking (900 rpm). Addition of LB is not required. 01:00:00
Plate on agar plates containing the appropriate antibiotic
creator: Alice Pawlowski
